Embedded firmware engineer. Bare-metal drivers, bootloader development, and real-time control systems on ARM/AVR/ESP32. Virginia Tech M.S. | IISc research background.

Rajashree Ramu · Electrical Engineer
01 Projects
P.01 — FLAGSHIP PROJECT
ALPHA dq — Custom Embedded Platform for Remote Firmware Development
Indian Institute of Science · Board Bring-Up & Firmware Stack

Developed at the Department of Electronic Systems Engineering, Indian Institute of Science under Prof. L. Umanand

A custom embedded platform for remote algorithm development enabling closed-loop feedback control across home automation, XY plotters, AC motor inverters, stepper motors, and wireless audio control. Built from schematic to a fully functional firmware stack.

  • Developed bottom-up peripheral drivers in C for UART, ADC, PWM, SPI, and GPIO using memory-mapped register access
    • Peripheral Driver Code
  • Implemented ESP32 firmware on ESP-IDF with FreeRTOS for wireless communication to ATmega328P.Integrated WPA2-Enterprise PEAP authentication and HTTP server on FreeRTOS ESP32, enabling secure remote algorithm deployment over enterprise WiFi without physical access to hardware
  • Built reusable peripheral library and environment setup toolchain
  • Debugged board behavior using USART logging, and oscilloscope across each bring-up stage
ATmega328PESP32ESP-IDFFreeRTOSUARTADCPWMSPIJTAGBoard Bring-Up
P.02
Custom USB Bootloader
IISc · ATmega328P

Custom bootloader enabling MCU programming directly over USB — eliminating the USB-to-Serial converter entirely.

  • Bit-banging V-USB stack to handle USB protocol without dedicated hardware
  • Interrupt Vector Table manipulation for clean boot-mode ↔ application switching
  • Reduced BOM and simplified production programming workflow
V-USBBit-BangingBootloaderInterrupt VectorsATmega328P
P.03
Sinusoidal PWM Inverter Control
IISc · Real-Time Power Electronics
Project Link

Sinusoidal PWM switching algorithm for high-power inverters controlling AC motors with deterministic real-time execution.

  • Direct 16-bit timer register manipulation for PWM and dead-time insertion
  • Hardware-level thermal protection under fault conditions
  • Deterministic performance for closed-loop motor control
SPWMTimer RegistersDead-TimeMotor ControlReal-Time
P.04
Real-Time Scheduling (FreeRTOS)
Virginia Tech · RTOS Systems

Implementation and benchmarking of scheduling algorithms for deterministic multi-task execution.

  • Implemented RM, DM, and EDF schedulers
  • Priority Ceiling Protocol to reduce worst-case blocking times
  • Benchmarked latency, blocking, and CPU utilization across policies
FreeRTOSRM / DM / EDFPCPSemaphoresMutexes
P.05
Fault Injection & Computer Architecture
Virginia Tech · Hardware Security

Hardware security research via voltage-glitch fault injection and TLB modeling for x86.

  • Voltage-glitch injection inducing instruction skipping and data corruption
  • Benchmarked fault effects on control flow and arithmetic on TM32
  • Contributed to published physical attack countermeasures research
Fault InjectionHardware SecurityTLB Modelingx86
P.06
Linux Kernel Programming
Virginia Tech · Systems Programming

Kernel-space CPU profiling, page table introspection, and custom system call implementation.

  • CPU profiling module with kprobes, hash tables, RB-trees via /proc
  • Page Table Walker for virtual-to-physical mapping inspection
  • Custom system calls implemented from scratch
Linux KernelkprobesPage TableSystem CallsRB-Tree
P.07
5G Stack Testing & VANET
Tetcos LLP · Network Simulation

Test engineering for 5G PHY layer in NetSim and VANET application development.

  • Test cases for 5G PHY layer validation in NetSim 5G Stack
  • AODV route table generation from protocol API specs
  • Custom vehicle-to-vehicle communication app for VANET module

Electrical Engineer
